LiquidPart.Liquid:
{% zone "Header" %}

Getting Started

Find the resources you need to make you productive

Documentation

Browse the documentation.

Gallery

Explore our gallery of module and themes.

Forum

Exchange with our friendly community.

Source

Explore the source code.

Download

Download the latest version.

Podcast

Join our public podcast every Tuesday at 12pm PT.

Portfolio

Some examples of websites proudly created using Orchard.

Vodaphone Freezone

Communication

Domino's UK Corporate

Corporate

LA Times Media Kit

News

Office Dev Center

Technology

Petit Bateau

Retail

Lexus

Automobile

Features

What makes Orchard special?

  • Media Management

    Define custom media types and add custom metadata like taxonomies, copyright, author information and more.

  • Multi-Lingual

    The content can be translated in any language, and elements of information can also be shared across languages.

  • Modular

    Every feature is a module which can be enabled, disabled or replaced. If you don't like what you see you can replace it with your own feature.

  • Multi-Tenant

    A single installation lets you manage multiple sites. The notion of Recipe lets you write reusable websites templates.

  • Security

    Define roles and permissions for all your users. Track changes and see the history of your content.

  • Workflows

    Create content workflow or react to events.

Partners

Nothing can be achieved alone

{% endzone %}

data.DisplayText: Workshop 1 – Deploy Orchard Core Website in Azure
data.Author: admin

HtmlBodyPart.Html:
• Set-up an OC development environment
  • Deploy to Azure
  • Enable Tenants (SaaS server, and Agency tenant)
  • Enable Azure Data Protection
  • Azure Pipelines / DevOps for deployment
  • Azure Sql Server
  • Enable Azure Blob Media Storage with Tenants
  • Discuss Media Caching / ImageSharp / Image Resizing considerations
  • How to add new image sizes and file formats
  • Enable Azure CDN for media and resource scripts
  • Enable Email (requires SendGrid, or SMTP server (registration emails)
  • Enable Sitemap
  • Considerations

TitlePart.Title: Workshop 1 – Deploy Orchard Core Website in Azure

HtmlBodyPart.Html:
Prerequisites:

- You know Git and you have a GitHub account (to retrieve the source code).

- You already tested Orchard Core and tried to setup a site (from source code or Code templates).

Summary of Topics to be discussed:

- Set-up an OC development environment.

- Create a Theme based on https://startbootstrap.com/templates/modern-business/

- Make new content types: Landing page, Portfolio, Blog.

- Use Liquid templates to control the display.

- Enable Indexing, Search and Results.

- Add Contact form with Workflows.

- Export your site in a recipe.

data.DisplayText: Workshop 3 – Using the Admin UI of Orchard Core
data.Author: admin

HtmlBodyPart.Html:
• Set-up an OC development environment
  • Use latest dev branch, not RC1
  • Using recipes, the content of the recipes, predefined contents/themes comes with the different kind of recipes
  • Content management, creating custom content types/content parts/content fields/content field editors, using the Widget stereotype (what is a widget?)
  • Named content parts like the BagPart, how to use the FlowPart, adding widgets to it
  • Layers, layer rules, zones
  • Templating using the admin UI, where you can write Liquid and see the live preview of your changes
  • How to enable/disable modules and themes
  • OpenID server, OpenID application, JWT token, to use the content APIs of Orchard Core, user management, permission management (creating a custom role)
  • Deployment plans, deployment step, check the structure of the Recipe.json file, here we could also mention the migrations
  • Ways to add content to Orchard

TitlePart.Title: Workshop 3 – Using the Admin UI of Orchard Core

HtmlBodyPart.Html:
• Objective: Add a new Orchard Core module (example:To-do lists -> work to-dos, home to-dos)
  • Set-up an OC development environment
  • Different ways of creating an empty module (Startup.cs file, Manifest.cs file)
  • Code Generation templates (https://docs.orchardcore.net/en/dev/docs/getting-started/templates/)
  • Implementing custom content parts/content types/content fields using the data migrations
  • Indexing the custom parts, YesSQL queries
  • Razor views, custom Razor Tag Helpers
  • Add a custom Razor Tag Helper, focus on Razor templates (.cshtml files)
  • AdminControllers (add custom site settings), admin menus, controllers
  • How to open pull request to contribute your module back to Orchard Core via GitHub

Summary of Topics to be discussed:

  • Who is the Commerce module for?
  • A quick tour of a commerce site - why so hard?
    • What is a product?
    • The process of buying stuff
    • The many processes of selling stuff
    • So many "details": payment, taxes, shipping, inventory, currencies, addresses, reporting, customers, recommendations, promotions, discounts, subscriptions
  • Building a catalog
  • Building a checkout process
  • Building an order fulfilment workflow
  • Theming a commerce site
  • Extending Orchard Core Commerce

Register form for the Workshops

The last Orchard conference, Orchard Harvest, was in New York in 2017. Based on the current economic situation and personal feedback, we will be holding our first online Orchard Harvest starting on the 31st of May at 1 PM UTC. If you're unsure when that is in your local time zone, check out World Time Buddy.

Just like the last Harvest, it’s going to be a great opportunity to share knowledge, talk about development plans and ideas, and foremost, meet the rest of the worldwide community.

Register for Orchard Harvest 2023 for free

Do you have questions about the conference, or ideas for it?

Take part in the discussion

Speakers

Andrii Chebukin

F# tech lead in Lula Technologies Inc., co-founder of The Secret Circle Solutions, lecturer in IT Step computer academy, Azure architect, maintainer of Elmish.Uno F# framework for Uno Platform, co-author of Surface Dial Tools and GuidFac extensions for Visual Studio.

Dávid El-Saig

A software engineer at Lombiq and the project leader of the Orchard Core Commerce development team.

Hisham Bin Ateya

Core contributor of Orchard Core, maintainer of Orchard Core Contrib and lecturer at Al-Rayan & Al-Arab Universities. His main expertise is localization. Hisham did massive localization contributions in many OSS such ASP.NET Core, ASP.NET Docs, Orchard Core, Oqtane Framework and SimplCommerce, etc.

Peyton McManus

Founder and president of Instant Technologies. Instant technologies has been providing IM, and chat based, enterprise applications since 2001. Instant has developed more than 10 enterprise applications across a variety of platforms (Sametime, Notes, Skype, Slack, MS Teams, O365). Prior to starting Instant, Peyton was a product manager, and developer, at Lotus\IBM including an onsite assignment at SAP.

Sébastien Ros

Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Sipke Schoorstra

Sipke Schoorstra is a software developer with a focus on .NET software development and architecture. Between 2010 and 2016, Sipke made valuable contributions to the Orchard project. Additionally, he is known as the creator of Elsa Workflows, an open-source .NET library for creating workflows-enabled applications.

Zoltán Lehóczky

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Program

Please welcome the below selection of awesome talks by community members. Your host for the day will be Viktória Magyar, who's the main organizer of Harvest.

After each session, we'll have a few minutes of Q&A, and then a quick 5-minute break.

All sessions will be recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel after the event, so even if you can't make it live, you'll be able to see the sessions. However, being there live will allow you to ask the speakers, as well as talk with other community members during the Breakout Sessions.

  • Opening

    Welcome

    1:00 PM-1:05 PM UTC

    We get everyone settled and open the conference.

  • Keynote

    1:05 PM-1:55 PM UTC

    Mostly jokes, not sure which ones yet.

  • Using Orchard Core Framework for multi-tenancy apps

    2:00 PM-2:40 PM UTC

    What is muti-tenancy? How doest it work in Orchard Core? Setting up another tenant. Per-tenant settings and services. Configuring the tenant URL. How to call one tenant from another one?

  • Automated QA for Orchard Core apps

    2:45 PM-3:40 PM UTC

    Are you sure your Orchard Core app is still working after the last commit? Manual testing is always necessary, but automated Quality Assurance is a must. In this talk, we’ll go through the lines of defense that you can utilize to ensure that your app doesn’t break. You’ll see how you can use open-source tools in the following areas: CI builds, static code analysis, unit and UI testing, telemetry collection.

  • Leveraging Orchard to rebuild, and reimagine, our enterprise service desk application

    3:45 PM-4:40 PM UTC

    18 months ago, I decided that our chat based service desk application required an overhaul. Our new application would be multi-tenant, SAAS, include a CMS, and enable us to quickly onboard new customers. This session will discuss why we selected Orchard, how our team has leverage Orchard to rebuild, and re-imagine, our application, and how we will continue to leverage Orchard for our next set of features. Our current customers are large, multi-national corporations with employees and service desk agents across multiple countries, languages, and continents.

  • Breakout

    Breakout Sessions

    4:45 PM-5:45 PM UTC

    Your chance to meet the speakers and other community members. Each speaker will have a breakout room where you can ask questions and discuss the topic of their talk, or anything else Orchard Core related.

  • Orchard Core Localization from Zero to Hero

    5:50 PM-7:00 PM UTC

    This talk will explore different parts of localization stuff in Orchard Core. We'll go through the process of how to make the app localization ready, then extract and manage the localization resources then using various localization services and UI components. After that, we'll look how to localize the content of your website. Finally, we might cover some of the future features including data localization.

  • E-Commerce with Orchard Core

    7:05 PM-8:00 PM UTC

    Orchard Core Commerce is the e-commerce solution or Orchard Core. It's the spiritual successor to Nwazet Commerce for Orchard 1. This talk will describe the project's state and demo the current features.

  • Elsa Workflows

    8:05 PM-9:00 PM UTC

    Join me for an exploration of Elsa Workflows, an innovative workflow engine derived from Orchard Core's Workflows module. This session will provide an overview of Elsa's features, compare it to OC Workflows, and discuss integration possibilities. Discover Elsa's visual designer, code-based workflows, versioning capabilities, composite activities, and sub workflows. Uncover the similarities and differences between OC and Elsa Workflows, and learn about Elsa's origins as an independent class library project. Gain insights into seamlessly integrating Elsa back into Orchard Core, combining the best of both worlds. Join me as we unlock the potential of Elsa Workflows and enhance Orchard Core with its remarkable capabilities.

  • Closing

The Organizer Team

The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2023 consists of long-time Orchard community members working on providing you with a great event.

  • Antoine Griffard
  • Benedek Farkas
  • Hisham Bin Ateya
  • Mike Alhayek
  • Sébastien Ros
  • Sotiris Roussos
  • Szymon Seliga
  • Viktória Magyar
  • Zoltán Lehóczky

LiquidPart.Liquid:
Orchard Harvest 2024 - The Orchard Core Conference

The Orchard Harvest conference will be in The Orleans Hotel & Casino, Las Vegas, on September 12th and 13th 2024.
Just like the last Harvest, it's going to be a great opportunity to share knowledge, talk about development plans and ideas, and foremost, meet the rest of the worldwide community.

See the program

We've prepared an exciting program for you, with talks from renowned community members and other sessions to make Orchard Harvest the biggest Orchard Core event of the year. Check out the program below!

Get your tickets

You can register to the conference using this link.
This ticket grants you two days of all-access to Orchard Harvest 2024.

Book your hotel

To book your stay at The Orleans Hotel & Casino, visit this page. Discounted booking is unfortunately now closed.

Contact the team

If you have questions, contact the organizer team.

Stay up to date

Do you have questions about the conference, or ideas for it? Visit the discussion board. Subscribe to the newsletter to know about updates right away.

Get hyped!

To give you a bit of a Harvest feeling, although from an online conference, check out last year's videos.

Speakers

Our awesome speakers are renowned members of the community and here to bring you the Orchard Core topics your care

Benedek Farkas

 GitHub

Orchard community member since 2011 and core contributor for more than a decade. Co-Founder and Managing Director of Lombiq Technologies, university guest lecturer since 2014. Full-stack and .NET developer and architect with multiple Orchard-based projects under his belt as a lead developer.

Burke Holland

 GitHub

Burke Holland is a front-end developer living in Nashville, TN; the greatest city in the world according to science. He enjoys JavaScript a lot because it's the only way he Node to Express himself. Get it? Never mind. Burke blogs only slightly better than he codes and definitely not as good as he talks about himself in the third person. Burke works with the VS Code team at Microsoft. You can find him on Twitter as @burkeholland.

Bruno Samardzic

 GitHub

Bruno Samardzic is a senior .NET developer and entrepreneur who enjoys working with Orchard Core. He's implemented a number of systems on top of Orchard, including the a main website of one of the leading courier services in the region, IOT hubs and user portals. Been using orchard since v1 onwards.

Drew Brasher

 GitHub

Drew is a Software Development Manager with 10 years of experience in Software Development. She is excited about Orchard Core and has used it both in her professional work and personal projects like her blog drewbrasher.com. When not creating software or learning cyber security, she enjoys spending time with her wife and children, watching shows/movies, hiking, walking, kayaking, bicycling, and fireside conversations.

Michal Kužela

 GitHub

Backend developer, Team & Tech lead in BiQ pux a.s.

Mike Alhayek

 GitHub

As a software engineer, I have a passion for collaborating with the Microsoft Stack, specializing in ASP.NET Core and Orchard Core.

Sebastien Ros

 GitHub

Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Sipke Schoorstra

 GitHub

Since 2000, Sipke has specialized in designing software solutions with a focus on the .NET ecosystem and cloud computing, working on diverse projects nationally and internationally. His career as a freelance developer was spearheaded by extensive work on Orchard projects, leading to his current deep involvement with Elsa Workflows, an open-source .NET project aimed at simplifying workflow creation. Beyond work, Sipke enjoys hiking, mountain biking, reading, gaming, and exploring blockchain technology's potential to transform industries and society.

Toby Dodds

 GitHub

Toby Dodds is a career web professional based at the Smithsonian Institution in Washington, D.C. As the Director of Web and IT he oversees the technical well-being of the Smithsonian Folkways website and digital collection. He has helped implement numerous innovations over his career and is a lifelong music enthusiast and avid photographer. He holds an undergraduate degree in philosophy from the University of Washington in Seattle, and a Masters in Library and Information Science degree from Catholic University in Washington, D.C.

Zoltán Lehóczky

 GitHub

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq. His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Sponsors

Companies that support the Orchard Core community and the Harvest conference

Program

We've prepared an exciting program for you, with talks from renowned community members and other sessions to make Orchard Harvest the biggest Orchard Core event of the year.

Your host for the day will be Viktória Magyar, who's the main organizer of Harvest.

All sessions will be recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel after the event, so even if you can't make it live, you'll be able to see the sessions. However, being there live will allow you to ask the speakers, meet other community members, and have a lot of fun!

All indicated times are Las Vegas local time. After each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions, and we'll have a short break too.

Day 1

  • Registration

    Registration, Coffee, and Breakfast Snacks

    8:00 AM - 9:00 AM

    Leisurely arrive at the venue, grab your badge, and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Opening

    Opening

    9:00 AM - 9:05 AM

    We officially open Orchard Harvest 2024.

  • Keynote

    9:05 AM - 9:30 AM

    Orchard Core as it is today, a review of what happened since last year, milestones and big events, what's in focus now, and what do we have planned for the foreseeable future.

  • Award

    Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

    9:30 AM - 9:40 AM

    Every year, the community votes on who has done the most for Orchard Core and thus who we can bestow the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award on.

  • Authentication in Orchard Core

    9:50 AM - 11:00 AM

    Join us for "Authentication in Orchard Core" to explore secure authentication and authorization within the Orchard Core framework. Learn about Single-Factor and Two-Factor Authentication, integrating external providers, and implementing OpenID Connect for enhanced security and user experience. This session will equip developers and IT professionals with the knowledge to secure their Orchard Core applications effectively.

  • Demystifying Shapes

    11:15 AM - 12:05 AM

    Shapes are the arcane magic of Orchard Core's theming. Or are they? While they are awesome, there's no magic, and by the end of this session, you'll fully understand them too!

  • Break

    Lunch Break

    12:05 AM - 1:35 PM

    Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

  • Content Warning Module Demo

    1:35 PM - 2:10 PM

    The Content Warning module lets you hide some content behind a warning so that users can decide if they want to view it or not. In this session, you will get to see it in action and take a look at the code that makes it happen. We will see how this is done with a Shortcode as well as a Content Part that adds a wrapper to the parent Content Item.

  • Panel

    Panel Discussion

    2:20 PM - 3:20 PM

    Submit your questions in advance or bring surprise questions and engage with our expert panel. Mike Alhayek, Toby Dodds, and Burke Holland will be there to answer your questions. Moderated by Sebastien Ros.

  • Break

    Coffee Break and Snacks

    3:20 PM - 3:40 PM

    Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Using Orchard as a Central Portal for the Biggest Small Business Association in SE Europe

    3:40 PM - 4:15 PM

    This talk is a showcase of how we used Orchard to implement this portal and what problems we encountered in the meanwhile. It's now been running for 2 years. What we feel worked, what could be better, and what hacks we resorted to.

  • From Zero to Azure: Setting Up Your Orchard Application for Success

    4:25 PM - 5:20 PM

    In this talk I'll go through a complete process of setting up an Azure environment tailored for Orchard, enhanced by various open-source components to aid developers and maintainers.

  • Closing

    Closing

    5:20 PM

    We end the first day of Orchard Harvest 2024.

  • Dinner

    Social Event - Dinner

    7:30 PM - 9:00 PM

    Join us for our social event for a relaxed evening! The location will be Orleans Hotel LaSalle B.

Day 2

  • Coffee

    Coffee and Breakfast Snacks

    8:00 AM - 8:30 AM

    No need to register this time, just arrive at the venue and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Building the Orchard Core Community

    8:30 AM - 9:00 AM

    Over the course of the last year or so, we've seen numerous improvements to how we function as a community. In this talk, we'll go through what has happened, what effects certain changes had on our community life, and a vision for what we'll see next.

  • Understanding the Performance Characteristics of Orchard Core

    9:10 AM - 10:05 AM

    Why is Orchard Core slow when it's slow, why it's fast when it's fast, and how can you figure all of this out? From the performance guy of the ASP.NET Core team.

  • Smithsonian Folkways Collection Management and Orchard Core Demonstration

    10:20 AM - 10:55 AM

    With a mission to increase and diffuse knowledge, Smithsonian Folkways Recordings digitizes and distributes a large catalog of historic sound recordings. At the middle of our enterprise is an Orchard Core implementation that has been customized to support the needs of our collection and website. By blending the role of website CMS with a robust collections management platform, Folkways has achieved its goals of broadly distributing its collection via its website and through multiple 3rd party music services and platforms - reaching millions of listeners per year.

  • Unlocking Hyperproductivity with Orchard, Elsa, and Intelligent Agents

    11:10 AM - 12:05 PM

    In this session, we will explore the integration of Orchard Core, Elsa Workflows, and intelligent agents to revolutionize content management. You will learn how to harness the power of AI to automate and enhance various stages of content creation and publishing using workflows and agents, achieving unprecedented productivity.

  • Break

    Lunch Break

    12:05 PM - 1:35 PM

    Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

  • Security Scanning an Orchard Core Application

    1:35 PM - 2:10 PM

    As a web developer, you have tons of security aspects to keep in mind: preventing XSS and SQL injection, configuring HTTPS and HSTS, setting up the proper CSP headers… While you do need to understand these, you have help doing them right: security scanning! In this talk, I'll show you how you can scan your Orchard Core app for security issues with Zed Attack Proxy, also doing it automatically as part of UI testing.

  • Speeding Up: Custom Development

    2:20 PM - 3:05 PM

    The talk's focus is on speeding up custom development via modules that bring new possibilities to Orchard Core. We have developed a module for creation & migration of DB tables, a module to fill these tables via CSV/Excel import and a module that shows them in dynamic tables with filters, sorters and more. All editable in admin, (almost) no-code required, so developers can focus on more complex tasks. Still a WIP though.

  • Hackathon

    Hackathon

    3:15 PM - 5:15 PM

    Collaborate and innovate with fellow attendees over coffee and snacks. We'll bring some Orchard Core issues that you'll be able to work on with fellow community members, for all skill levels.

  • Closing

    Closing

    5:15 PM

    We end the second day of Orchard Harvest 2024.

The Organizer Team

The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2024 consists of long-time Orchard community members working on providing you with a great event.

Antoine Griffard
Benedek Farkas
Hisham Bin Ateya
Mike Alhayek
Sébastien Ros
Viktória Magyar
Zoltán Lehóczky

Sponsors

Companies that support the Orchard Core community and the Harvest conference.

{% comment %}
{% endcomment %}

Program

We're preparing an exciting program for you, with talks from renowned community members and other sessions to make Orchard Harvest the biggest Orchard Core event of the year.

{% comment %} Your host for the day will be Viktória Magyar, who's the main organizer of Harvest.

All sessions will be recorded and published on the Orchard YouTube channel after the event, so even if you can't make it live, you'll be able to see the sessions. However, being there live will allow you to ask the speakers, meet other community members, and have a lot of fun!

All indicated times are Las Vegas local time. After each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions, and we'll have a short break too.

Day 1

  • Registration

    Registration, Coffee, and Breakfast Snacks

    8:00 AM - 9:00 AM

    Leisurely arrive at the venue, grab your badge, and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Opening

    Opening

    9:00 AM - 9:05 AM

    We officially open Orchard Harvest 2024.

  • Keynote

    9:05 AM - 9:30 AM

    Orchard Core as it is today, a review of what happened since last year, milestones and big events, what's in focus now, and what do we have planned for the foreseeable future.

  • Award

    Announcement of the Winner of the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award

    9:30 AM - 9:40 AM

    Every year, the community votes on who has done the most for Orchard Core and thus who we can bestow the Jean-Thierry Kéchichian Community Award on.

  • Authentication in Orchard Core

    9:50 AM - 11:00 AM

    Join us for "Authentication in Orchard Core" to explore secure authentication and authorization within the Orchard Core framework. Learn about Single-Factor and Two-Factor Authentication, integrating external providers, and implementing OpenID Connect for enhanced security and user experience. This session will equip developers and IT professionals with the knowledge to secure their Orchard Core applications effectively.

  • Demystifying Shapes

    11:15 AM - 12:05 AM

    Shapes are the arcane magic of Orchard Core's theming. Or are they? While they are awesome, there's no magic, and by the end of this session, you'll fully understand them too!

  • Break

    Lunch Break

    12:05 AM - 1:35 PM

    Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

  • Content Warning Module Demo

    1:35 PM - 2:10 PM

    The Content Warning module lets you hide some content behind a warning so that users can decide if they want to view it or not. In this session, you will get to see it in action and take a look at the code that makes it happen. We will see how this is done with a Shortcode as well as a Content Part that adds a wrapper to the parent Content Item.

  • Panel

    Panel Discussion

    2:20 PM - 3:20 PM

    Submit your questions in advance or bring surprise questions and engage with our expert panel. Mike Alhayek, Toby Dodds, and Burke Holland will be there to answer your questions. Moderated by Sebastien Ros.

  • Break

    Coffee Break and Snacks

    3:20 PM - 3:40 PM

    Mingle with other community members and grab a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Using Orchard as a Central Portal for the Biggest Small Business Association in SE Europe

    3:40 PM - 4:15 PM

    This talk is a showcase of how we used Orchard to implement this portal and what problems we encountered in the meanwhile. It's now been running for 2 years. What we feel worked, what could be better, and what hacks we resorted to.

  • From Zero to Azure: Setting Up Your Orchard Application for Success

    4:25 PM - 5:20 PM

    In this talk I'll go through a complete process of setting up an Azure environment tailored for Orchard, enhanced by various open-source components to aid developers and maintainers.

  • Closing

    Closing

    5:20 PM

    We end the first day of Orchard Harvest 2024.

  • Dinner

    Social Event - Dinner

    7:30 PM - 9:00 PM

    Join us for our social event for a relaxed evening! The location will be Orleans Hotel LaSalle B.

Day 2

  • Coffee

    Coffee and Breakfast Snacks

    8:00 AM - 8:30 AM

    No need to register this time, just arrive at the venue and mingle with other community members with a cup of coffee and some snacks.

  • Building the Orchard Core Community

    8:30 AM - 9:00 AM

    Over the course of the last year or so, we've seen numerous improvements to how we function as a community. In this talk, we'll go through what has happened, what effects certain changes had on our community life, and a vision for what we'll see next.

  • Understanding the Performance Characteristics of Orchard Core

    9:10 AM - 10:05 AM

    Why is Orchard Core slow when it's slow, why it's fast when it's fast, and how can you figure all of this out? From the performance guy of the ASP.NET Core team.

  • Smithsonian Folkways Collection Management and Orchard Core Demonstration

    10:20 AM - 10:55 AM

    With a mission to increase and diffuse knowledge, Smithsonian Folkways Recordings digitizes and distributes a large catalog of historic sound recordings. At the middle of our enterprise is an Orchard Core implementation that has been customized to support the needs of our collection and website. By blending the role of website CMS with a robust collections management platform, Folkways has achieved its goals of broadly distributing its collection via its website and through multiple 3rd party music services and platforms - reaching millions of listeners per year.

  • Unlocking Hyperproductivity with Orchard, Elsa, and Intelligent Agents

    11:10 AM - 12:05 PM

    In this session, we will explore the integration of Orchard Core, Elsa Workflows, and intelligent agents to revolutionize content management. You will learn how to harness the power of AI to automate and enhance various stages of content creation and publishing using workflows and agents, achieving unprecedented productivity.

  • Break

    Lunch Break

    12:05 PM - 1:35 PM

    Visit one of the many restaurants and bars of The Orleans and enjoy a meal with the other community members. Please note that lunch is not included in the conference ticket.

  • Security Scanning an Orchard Core Application

    1:35 PM - 2:10 PM

    As a web developer, you have tons of security aspects to keep in mind: preventing XSS and SQL injection, configuring HTTPS and HSTS, setting up the proper CSP headers… While you do need to understand these, you have help doing them right: security scanning! In this talk, I'll show you how you can scan your Orchard Core app for security issues with Zed Attack Proxy, also doing it automatically as part of UI testing.

  • Speeding Up: Custom Development

    2:20 PM - 3:05 PM

    The talk's focus is on speeding up custom development via modules that bring new possibilities to Orchard Core. We have developed a module for creation & migration of DB tables, a module to fill these tables via CSV/Excel import and a module that shows them in dynamic tables with filters, sorters and more. All editable in admin, (almost) no-code required, so developers can focus on more complex tasks. Still a WIP though.

  • Hackathon

    Hackathon

    3:15 PM - 5:15 PM

    Collaborate and innovate with fellow attendees over coffee and snacks. We'll bring some Orchard Core issues that you'll be able to work on with fellow community members, for all skill levels.

  • Closing

    Closing

    5:15 PM

    We end the second day of Orchard Harvest 2024.

{% endcomment %}

The Organizer Team

The team organizing Orchard Harvest 2024 consists of long-time Orchard community members working on providing you with a great event.

Antoine Griffard
Benedek Farkas
Hisham Bin Ateya
Mike Alhayek
Sébastien Ros
Viktória Magyar
Zoltán Lehóczky

HtmlBodyPart.Html:
Orchard Harvest 2024 - The Orchard Core Conference

The Orchard Harvest conference will be in The Orleans Hotel & Casino, Las Vegas, on September 12th and 13th 2024.
Just like the last Harvest, it's going to be a great opportunity to share knowledge, talk about development plans and ideas, and foremost, meet the rest of the worldwide community.

See the program

We've prepared an exciting program for you, with talks from renowned community members and other sessions to make Orchard Harvest the biggest Orchard Core event of the year. Check out the program below!

Get your tickets

You can register to the conference using this link.
This ticket grants you two days of all-access to Orchard Harvest 2024.

Book your hotel

To book your stay at The Orleans Hotel & Casino, visit this page. Discounted booking is unfortunately now closed.

Contact the team

If you have questions, contact the organizer team.

Stay up to date

Do you have questions about the conference, or ideas for it? Visit the discussion board. Subscribe to the newsletter to know about updates right away.

Get hyped!

To give you a bit of a Harvest feeling, although from an online conference, check out last year's videos.

Speakers

Our awesome speakers are renowned members of the community and here to bring you the Orchard Core topics your care

Benedek Farkas

 GitHub

Orchard community member since 2011 and core contributor for more than a decade. Co-Founder and Managing Director of Lombiq Technologies, university guest lecturer since 2014. Full-stack and .NET developer and architect with multiple Orchard-based projects under his belt as a lead developer.

Burke Holland

 GitHub

Burke Holland is a front-end developer living in Nashville, TN; the greatest city in the world according to science. He enjoys JavaScript a lot because it's the only way he Node to Express himself. Get it? Never mind. Burke blogs only slightly better than he codes and definitely not as good as he talks about himself in the third person. Burke works with the VS Code team at Microsoft. You can find him on Twitter as @burkeholland.

Bruno Samardzic

 GitHub

Bruno Samardzic is a senior .NET developer and entrepreneur who enjoys working with Orchard Core. He's implemented a number of systems on top of Orchard, including the a main website of one of the leading courier services in the region, IOT hubs and user portals. Been using orchard since v1 onwards.

Drew Brasher

 GitHub

Drew is a Software Development Manager with 10 years of experience in Software Development. She is excited about Orchard Core and has used it both in her professional work and personal projects like her blog drewbrasher.com. When not creating software or learning cyber security, she enjoys spending time with her wife and children, watching shows/movies, hiking, walking, kayaking, bicycling, and fireside conversations.

Michal Kužela

 GitHub

Backend developer, Team & Tech lead in BiQ pux a.s.

Mike Alhayek

 GitHub

As a software engineer, I have a passion for collaborating with the Microsoft Stack, specializing in ASP.NET Core and Orchard Core.

Sebastien Ros

 GitHub

Developer at Microsoft, benevolent dictator of Orchard Core.

Sipke Schoorstra

 GitHub

Since 2000, Sipke has specialized in designing software solutions with a focus on the .NET ecosystem and cloud computing, working on diverse projects nationally and internationally. His career as a freelance developer was spearheaded by extensive work on Orchard projects, leading to his current deep involvement with Elsa Workflows, an open-source .NET project aimed at simplifying workflow creation. Beyond work, Sipke enjoys hiking, mountain biking, reading, gaming, and exploring blockchain technology's potential to transform industries and society.

Toby Dodds

 GitHub

Toby Dodds is a career web professional based at the Smithsonian Institution in Washington, D.C. As the Director of Web and IT he oversees the technical well-being of the Smithsonian Folkways website and digital collection. He has helped implement numerous innovations over his career and is a lifelong music enthusiast and avid photographer. He holds an undergraduate degree in philosophy from the University of Washington in Seattle, and a Masters in Library and Information Science degree from Catholic University in Washington, D.C.

Zoltán Lehóczky

 GitHub

Co-founder and managing director of Lombiq. His main expertise is in .NET software development and software architecture design. Active in open-source, core contributor of Orchard Core, guest lecturer at Óbuda University, John von Neumann Faculty of Informatics.

Sponsors

Companies that support the Orchard Core community and the Harvest conference

Layers.Description: The widgets in this layer are only displayed on the homepage.
